pi_items.source: setup_moodle.sh line 324: course created with shortname='HIST201' and fullname='World History'; setup_task.sh line 14 queries this course by shortname
setup_task.sh line 35 queries role with shortname='student'; Moodle documentation (https://docs.moodle.org/501/en/Roles_and_permissions) confirms 'student' is a standard predefined role

setup_task.sh lines 42 and export_result.sh line 23 use this exact capability name; Moodle documentation (https://docs.moodle.org/405/en/Capabilities/moodle/course:viewparticipants) confirms this is a real capability that allows viewing course participants

export_result.sh line 22 documents: 'permission values: 1 = Allow, -1 = Prevent, -1000 = Prohibit'; Moodle documentation (https://docs.moodle.org/501/en/Permissions) confirms -1 is the value for Prevent; verifier.py line 70 checks for this value

export_result.sh line 22 documents: 'permission values: 1 = Allow, -1 = Prevent, -1000 = Prohibit'; Moodle documentation (https://docs.moodle.org/501/en/Permissions) confirms -1000 is the value for Prohibit and notes it cannot be overridden; verifier.py line 73 checks for this value

privileged_info_summary: The task targets the HIST201 course (fullname: 'World History'), specifically modifying the 'student' role's permissions for the 'moodle/course:viewparticipants' capability at the course context level. The permission values are: -1 for Prevent (removes permission even if allowed in higher context) and -1000 for Prohibit (absolute denial that cannot be overridden). All data is synthetic, created during Moodle environment initialization with student enrollments and course structure predetermined.
